note: Curated replay of real DGX Spark runs. Answers + measured TTFT and throughput are real; per-token cadence is synthesized from the measured tok/s. No sidecar required.

chat.prompt: Draft a 3-bullet release note for the Spark Arena v0.1.1 cockpit overhaul.
In one short paragraph, what makes a DGX Spark well suited to running local language models?

Question: Tensor veli palatini is supplied by:

A) Facial nerve
B) Trigeminal nerve
C) Glossopharyngeal nerve
D) Pharyngeal plexus

Choose the correct answer. Reply with only the single letter A, B, C, or D.
Explain how the Spark Arena cockpit telemetry rail works in three lines.
create a table of tallest buildings

rubrics.title: Generic correctness
rubrics.description: Passes when the answer is non-empty and does not start with a hedge phrase — the floor rubric for free-form prompts where the operator hasn't picked a specialized rubric yet. Deterministic and fast.

rubrics.title: Patent claim validity
rubrics.description: Patent-strategist canonical rubric — looks for at least one of the claim-validity anchor terms (anticipation / obviousness / written description / enablement / 35 U.S.C. § 102 / § 103 / § 112). One of the three default rubrics so the patent vertical's compare runs produce comparable scores out of the box.

rubrics.title: MCQ letter (A/B/C/D)
rubrics.description: Cyber-bench canonical rubric — expects the final answer to contain a standalone 'A', 'B', 'C', or 'D' letter (whitespace- or punctuation-bounded), matching the multiple-choice format the cyber vertical bench uses. Per `feedback_keep_scorer_local_until_reuse`, this scorer was the first to ship beyond plain substring; promoted from the cyber preflight after the second-vertical-reuse gate.
